questions.question: Which of the following best describes the perceive-reason-act-observe loop?

options.A: A one-shot input-output mapping with no iteration
options.B: The defining operational cycle of an AI agent
options.C: A technique exclusive to reactive agents
options.D: A hardware architecture for neural networks

questions.question: What is the most fundamental difference between a chatbot and an AI agent?

options.B: Agents can only run on cloud infrastructure
options.C: Agents can use tools and take multi-step autonomous actions to change external state
options.D: Chatbots are always faster to respond

questions.question: A reactive agent is best characterised by which property?

options.A: It maintains a detailed world model and plans ahead
options.B: It reacts directly to current input without persistent internal state
options.C: It combines deliberative and reactive behaviours

questions.question: Which agent type is most appropriate for a research task that requires outlining a plan, searching multiple sources, and writing a report?
Most production AI agents (including Claude with tools) are classified as which type?
Which of the following is NOT a key property of an AI agent?
In the perceive-reason-act-observe loop, what happens during the 'Act' phase?

options.A: The agent receives input from its environment
options.B: The agent updates its internal world model
options.C: The agent calls a tool, writes a response, or requests more information
options.D: The agent decides what to do based on its goals

questions.question: Which scenario is best suited for an AI agent rather than a chatbot?

options.A: Answering 'What is the capital of France?'
options.B: Booking a flight, checking a calendar, and emailing an itinerary
options.D: Generating a single creative writing piece

questions.question: What is the primary advantage of a hybrid agent over a purely reactive agent?

options.C: Hybrid agents combine fast reactions for simple tasks with deliberative planning for complex ones
options.D: Hybrid agents require no system prompt

questions.question: Agent autonomy refers to the agent's ability to:

options.B: Make multi-step decisions without per-step human direction

questions.question: Which of the following best describes agent memory in the context of the perceive-reason-act-observe loop?

options.A: Permanent storage of all past conversations across all users
options.B: The agent's ability to maintain context of prior steps within the current session
options.C: A database that the agent queries for factual information
options.D: The model weights stored on disk

questions.question: A deliberative agent builds and updates an internal world model primarily to:

options.C: Plan sequences of actions and reason about future states before executing

questions.question: Which statement about agent tool use is most accurate?

options.A: Only agents with internet access can use tools
options.B: Tool use is what allows agents to modify external state beyond generating text
options.C: Tools are optional and agents perform better without them
options.D: Tools are limited to database queries

questions.question: An agent that labels each customer support message as positive or negative independently, without remembering previous messages, is an example of:
Which of the following is the most accurate description of how the perceive-reason-act-observe loop terminates?

options.B: It runs forever until the server is shut down
options.C: It runs until the agent determines the task is complete or a stopping condition is met
